## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On August 24, 79 AD, the absolute, most massive, incredibly devastating, and historically famous volcanic eruption in ancient history violently occurred. Mount Vesuvius aggressively and catastrophically erupted, spewing a massive, incredibly lethal column of ash and pumice high into the stratosphere. The massive, superheated pyroclastic flows completely annihilated and heavily buried the incredibly prosperous Roman cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum. The massive volcanic ash fiercely preserved the cities in an absolute, macabre time capsule, fundamentally providing unparalleled insight into ancient Roman society centuries later.

On August 24, 1572, the incredibly massive, horrifically bloody, and deeply tragic St. Bartholomew's Day massacre aggressively erupted in Paris, France. Instigated heavily by the incredibly powerful Catholic monarchy, heavily armed mobs violently and aggressively slaughtered thousands of massive French Calvinist Protestants (Huguenots) across the city and subsequently throughout the massive provinces. This incredible, massive religious atrocity absolutely shattered the French Protestant movement and heavily escalated the incredibly brutal, massive French Wars of Religion.

On August 24, 1414, Khizr Khan, the massive, incredibly strategic, and highly powerful military governor, officially and aggressively established the Sayyid dynasty.
